title: Evangelion 222 Rei was cooking for Shinji Was Asuka too
display_title: Evangelion 222 Rei was cooking for Shinji Was Asuka too
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.22 - Rei was cooking for Shinji. Was Asuka too?             Quote Post by Leighton » Sat Mar 05, 2011 3:41 am Which implies, are they both fighting for Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Mar 05, 2011 3:51 am Yeah, that's the implication. Although Rei is cooking for Shinji and Gendou, as opposed to cooking to have Shinji validate her sense of self-worth.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:31 am I do seem to remember Asuka wondering if Shinji would have preferred what she was cooking to be milder. So, yeah. She was cooking for Shinji. Also, what Azathoth said.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 05, 2011 8:28 am Asuka was doing it too You see her standing there in an apron wondering if it she should make it less spicy since it's for him, so she explicitly states it - Misato catches her and immediately grasps what going on. Misato even lampshades the occurrences by saying something like "It's a good sigh that both you and Rei have started showing intrest in boys!" (Of course it's a good singn that being EVA pilots has not kept them from having the developements that 14 year olds usually have, but I wonder why Misato doesn't see any problem with the fact that both of them are intrested in THE SAME boy....) Asuka never actually presents her creations to Shinji, even if she, being her usual ambitious self, gets the hang of it much faster than Rei ( she has significatly less cuts/burns/bandaids on her hands. ) She most likely gave up on winning Shinji over with food, given her horryfied reaction when Misato tells her that Rei is doing exactly the same thing and has already handed out the invitations. They are obviously competing for his heart, tough I doubt that Rei sees it as a competetion/is even aware that she has a Rival. (It is important to note that much unlike his Anime counterpart, Rebuild!Shinji has not shown any attraction to Asuka, tough he does seem to see her as a valuable friend post sleepover-scene, since he tells Gendo he needs to go and lose someone important, too (Which is very much harsher in hindsight. Poor Gendo.)) Asuka is clearly shown giving Rei and Shinji jealous stares throurough the first half of the movie, tough. Note that the cooking attempts and resulting bandaids are a metaphor/symbol for the girls' attitude towards Shinji here - Asuka wants to have Shinji for herself, but she takes too much and rarely gives (Okay, she doesn't want to open up/show her vulnerable side/depend on anyone, for reasons that are anything but her own fault but it is nonetheless the sad truth that she rarely wonders how her constant rejection makes Shinji feel), she cooks to "win him" like he was some sort of price. Rei on the other hand, doesn't just want to win him over for herself - she really wants to do something for him , i.e, fullfilling his greatest inner wish to get closer to his father, and the only thing she hopes to get in return is for him to "feel warm and content as well". This leads to the elevator scene, where Asuka compares their number of band-aids. It's not about the cuts themselves (That would be pretty stupid), but about what they represent: Only one of these girls loves Shinji more than herself. The Elevator scene fulfills the role of it's ep 22 equivalent for Asuka, but for Rei, it's the argument in ep 16. (The one where Misato actually tells both girls to stop arguing) There, Asuka was obviously also worried about Shinji (She shows up at the hospital later), but kept coldly insultig him tough he was presumed dead, even laughing at his misfortune, not because she actually didn't care, but because her pride comes before Shinji. (Other examples: Wants to talk to him about her stepmother but stops herself, insults him/calls him an idiot, tells him to stay out, but still crawls into his bed, wants to kiss him, but tells him that she's bored (implying that she's just experimenting, as many 14 year olds do) Or, Rebuild Examples: She likes Shinji's cooking(remember the metaphor! His efforts to please her, if not in a romantic way) so much that she wouldn't share it(literally, i.e, referring to the actual food, with hikari, following the metaphor, it's ei she doesn't want to share his attention with), but calls it 'mediocre'), she kicks Shinji in the face and calls him a pervert, but that kick required spreading her legs, causing Shinji to see more of her... girl parts than he would have seen if she had simply punched him. Or, a non-Shinji-related example concerning her attitude to other people as a whole, she's playing her video games, looking slightly lonely, but she's kicking her fanboys in the guts and watching them drop like domino stones.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Mar 05, 2011 11:51 am Well done, Kendrix. It all makes sense now on their hidden relationships. Let me get this straight: Asuka wants Shinji all for herself while Rei just wants to have a bonding relationship with him.

[]: Re: Evangelion 2.22 - Rei was cooking for Shinji. Was Asuka             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 05, 2011 12:00 pm Leighton wrote: Which implies, are they both fighting for Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Mar 05, 2011 12:24 pm Kendrix wrote: she kicks Shinji in the face and calls him a pervert, but that kick required spreading her legs, causing Shinji to see more of her... girl parts than he would have seen if she had simply punched him.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 05, 2011 5:43 pm A.T. Fish wrote: I don't think anyone would see anything but a feet, and then darkness, when taking that hit. Plus, it was all about the fanservice, not about showing us Asuka's feelings.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:27 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:46 pm Rei was peeing for Shinji. Was Asuka too? I think this important part, comes before the cooking so. Srsly though, I remember Asuka fail-cooking but someone enlighten me to Rei's one? When did this take place, can't recall at all.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:50 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Srsly though, I remember Asuka fail-cooking but someone enlighten me to Rei's one? When did this take place, can't recall at all.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Mar 05, 2011 7:53 pm EvangelionFan wrote: I believe it was implied that she began to start cooking after that shot of her raising the knife. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Sun Mar 06, 2011 2:01 am Azathoth wrote: Yeah, that's the implication. Although Rei is cooking for Shinji and Gendou, as opposed to cooking to have Shinji validate her sense of self-worth.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Mar 06, 2011 2:14 am Sun Stealer wrote: Isn't Rei also seeking to validate her self-worth by trying to reconcile Shinji and his father? Isn't helping improve the lives of loved ones a way for oneself to validate one's existence?

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Sun Mar 06, 2011 10:23 am Azathoth wrote: I would say she's trying to act selflessly, as a deliberate distraction from her own self-hatred and will to death - or put another way, as a method of submerging her own identity in someone else's.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 07, 2011 3:01 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: So a person taking simple action towards wanting to be with another person is nefarious. What a dark twisted world.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Sat Mar 12, 2011 1:42 am What must have happened was that Rei and Asuka went on Iron Chef to prove which one loved Shinji the most. Rather than the winner being the one who could cook better, the judges had to decide based on who could still continue cooking, and as Rei still had use of her thumbs; she was the winner. However they cut that scene from the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 12, 2011 5:41 am Sun Stealer wrote: What must have happened was that Rei and Asuka went on Iron Chef to prove which one loved Shinji the most. Rather than the winner being the one who could cook better, the judges had to decide based on who could still continue cooking, and as Rei still had use of her thumbs; she was the winner. However they cut that scene from the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Mar 12, 2011 1:04 pm Sun Stealer wrote: What must have happened was that Rei and Asuka went on Iron Chef to prove which one loved Shinji the most. Rather than the winner being the one who could cook better, the judges had to decide based on who could still continue cooking, and as Rei still had use of her thumbs; she was the winner. However they cut that scene from the movie.
